danger_signals.visual_form: Evil power entity (inferred from narrative framing)

danger_signals.visual_form: Named villain encounters with sinister design (contrasted with comical enemies)
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat encounters; faction-aligned minions signal dangerous story progression points

danger_signals.visual_form: Town created to house Homncruse victims (visual indicator of devastation zones)
danger_signals.meaning: Areas marked by refugee presence indicate recently affected/dangerous territories

safety_signals.meaning: Safe spaces for advancement; associated with power-granting rituals (Banalet ascension)

safety_signals.visual_form: Warp icons within temples leading to treasure dungeons
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled/managed danger; structured progression areas under player control
Safe origin point; departure from safety signals progression into wider world

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Victim settlements indicate Homncruse corruption zones; destroyed/devastated landscapes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dan's hereditary lineage (blood connection to power)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with Dan's exceptional warrior status; lore-based faction without visual markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Devastated landscapes; victim settlements; environmental decay in corrupted zones

environmental_storytelling.example: Player-created town housing Homncruse victims serves as visual story anchor; persistent evidence of antagonist threat
Hidden temples scattered worldwide signal lore distribution; architecture indicates sacred/safe spaces for power acquisition
Pennon → Banalet status transitions mark party member power levels; visual display of character advancement tied to narrative gates
Inaccessible areas without correct party composition; dungeon design enforces narrative party structure; world layout mirrors story progression
Comical villains (Cat Boo, Badbad) vs. sinister elite generals; visual enemy appearance conveys threat level and narrative tone

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Isolated on tiny island; solo adventure begins; gains party members as story progresses
Limited access to world areas; cannot proceed without Banalet advancement; restricted party switching
Expanded area access unlocked; enhanced capabilities; party switching enabled for area-specific puzzles
Unique power-bonding ability; unifies Aqutallion party; catalyst for final transformation
Supplement primary party; can substitute in combat; access exclusive Kustera-only dungeons via warp zones
Manifests through elite generals; corrupts regions; spawns comical and sinister minions; creates refugee populations
Provide tonal contrast; early-game encounters; visual/behavioral comedy markers
Story-critical battles; sinister visual design; progression gates; escalating difficulty markers

notes: Secret of the Stars employs a dual-party system where visual/geographic accessibility gates progression through party composition requirements. World-state manifestation is primarily narrative-driven with rank-based status (Pennon/Banalet) serving as visual progression markers. Faction identity relies on role definition (Aqutallions as warriors, Kustera as supplements, Homncruse as corruption force) rather than color or sigil systems. Environmental storytelling emphasizes settlement placement, temple sanctuary design, and enemy visual hierarchy. The game uses party separation mechanics as core world-state communication—inaccessible areas become available as parties gain Banalet status. Homncruse threat is manifested through environmental corruption (victim settlements, devastated landscapes) and enemy encounter design (tonal progression from comical to sinister). Data is qualitative due to T3 tier; visual design details inferred from gameplay mechanics and narrative structure documented in available sources.
